metadata.name: Codex ACP Precreation Verification Loop
metadata.description: Verify the Codex CLI ACP precreation path and stop before a real provider-backed Worker launch.

objective.user_goal: Keep Codex CLI integration claims bounded to verifier-backed local evidence.
objective.done_definition: Named non-production approval, model-resource credential reference, disposable Pod creation, ACP terminal evidence, provider smoke, and cleanup all pass.

ambiguity_triggers: approval does not name a model resource

secondary_user_query.prompt: May one named non-production organization model resource be used for a disposable Codex ACP Worker and then cleaned up?

block_if: a provider request would be sent without named approval
the model resource credential scope is unclear

decomposition_policy.strategy: goal-based evidence loop with a human live-launch gate
decomposition_policy.dependency_policy: live launch depends on applied template and named approval

loop_nodes.purpose: Revalidate Codex ACP precreation facts and stop at the credential-backed launch gate.

children.purpose: Reject stale contract, image, Runner, model-binding, browser, and database evidence.
entry_conditions: local Codex image and Runner exist

children.purpose: Require named approval before model-resource credential use, Pod creation, ACP use, provider smoke, and cleanup.

atomic_tasks.goal: Verify Codex CLI Definition, AgentFile, required model protocol, and credential source boundary.
acceptance_criteria: Codex uses codex-app-server with ACP.
Codex requires an openai-compatible model binding.

atomic_tasks.goal: Verify current Codex image, version, and connected Runner without launching a Pod.
acceptance_criteria: Catalog and Docker image share the current Codex digest.
Runner reports codex-cli 0.144.5 and a connected tunnel.

atomic_tasks.goal: Verify browser validation, plan, and template apply with a resolved required model binding.
acceptance_criteria: Template and launch validation and plans pass.
Snapshot pins qa-primary-model and no Worker launch or Pod exists.

atomic_tasks.goal: Independently confirm that the Codex record is precreation-only and that runtime preferences are not credentials.
acceptance_criteria: Review rejects a supported claim without live evidence.
Review confirms plaintext runtime preferences are not accepted as credential evidence.

atomic_tasks.goal: After named approval only, verify model-resource credential use, disposable Pod, ACP terminal, provider smoke, and cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Named organization model-resource approval is recorded.
Live launch, ACP terminal, provider result, and cleanup pass the live verifier.

disallowed_when: a task uses a provider credential or changes shared state
subagent_activation.token_budget_policy: Return evidence paths, commands, exit codes, and blockers only.

context_policy.tool_output_trimming: Retain commands, exit codes, facts, and evidence paths; exclude raw credentials and authorization headers.

compaction.summary_contract: Preserve Codex scope, evidence, verifier outcomes, approval state, and blockers.

success: live verifier passes after named approval
all five acceptance items are checked
failure: a protected verifier would need weakening
the approved disposable resource cannot be cleaned up

verifiers.expected_result: exit 0 only for a current Codex ACP precreation record blocked for live launch
verifiers.scope: contract, image, Runner, model binding, browser, database, and review evidence

verifiers.expected_result: exit 0 only after named approval and disposable lifecycle evidence exist
verifiers.scope: model-resource credential use, Pod, ACP terminal, provider smoke, and cleanup

anti_gaming_rules: Do not weaken verification or replace missing live evidence with a status claim.
Do not treat plaintext runtime preferences as credentials.

cost_policy.optimization_goal: Use deterministic local checks before any approved external operation.

user_confirmation.prompt: May the loop use one named non-production organization model resource for a disposable Codex ACP Worker and clean it up?

drift_checks: precreation is not promoted to support
runtime preferences are not credential evidence

escalation_required_when: a public contract change is needed

execution_adapters.approval_model: human approval required for live launch
execution_adapters.context_reload_model: reload state files at each cycle

portability_policy.platform_selection_query: Use Codex while this loop is local; regenerate adapters before moving runtimes.

failure_modes.mitigation: Refresh image and catalog facts before accepting runtime evidence.
Set blocked_human_gate and request named scope.
Stop and escalate; do not mark support.

acceptance_checklist.update_policy: Check an item only after its verifier-backed criteria and artifacts exist.

acceptance_criteria: Codex uses codex-app-server with ACP.
Codex requires an openai-compatible model binding.
Catalog and Docker image share the current Codex digest.
Runner reports codex-cli 0.144.5 and a connected tunnel.
Template and launch validation and plans pass.
Snapshot pins qa-primary-model and no Worker launch or Pod exists.
Review rejects a supported claim without live evidence.
Review confirms plaintext runtime preferences are not accepted as credential evidence.
Named organization model-resource approval is recorded.
Live launch, ACP terminal, provider result, and cleanup pass the live verifier.

escalation.condition: A human gate, cleanup failure, live verifier failure, or no-progress limit is reached.
escalation.message_template: Codex ACP loop stopped at {task_id}: {reason}. Evidence: {evidence_ref}.
